questions.question: What is the topic for Week 7?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Seven is FORGIVENESS.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 6?
questions.explanation: Know Yourself was the topic for Week 6.

questions.question: And He is before all things, and in Him all things ________.
questions.explanation: The memory verse (Colossians 1:17) says 'and in Him all things consist.'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse is explicitly listed as Colossians 1:17.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 7 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: And He is before all things, and in Him all things consist.
questions.explanation: Full text recall for Colossians 1:17.

questions.question: What two facts does the memory verse (Colossians 1:17) declare about 'Him'?
options: A. He is the Creator and the Redeemer
B. He is before all things, and in Him all things consist
C. He forgives sins and heals diseases
D. He is the Alpha and Omega
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states 'He is before all things, and in Him all things consist.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what is forgiveness?
options: A. Forgetting that an offense ever happened
B. An intentional decision to put aside feelings of resentment, anger, and revenge
C. Punishing the offender so you can feel better
D. Waiting until you get to heaven to let things go
questions.explanation: The introduction defines it as 'an intentional decision to put aside feelings of resentment, anger, and revenge...'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited in the introduction to warn that those who refuse to forgive men will not be forgiven by God?
questions.explanation: The text points to '(Matthew 6:14-15)' for this principle.

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline in the Week 7 lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'REASONS CHRISTIANS SHOULD FORGIVE'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, what is one of the fundamental obligations of a Christian? (Supported by Hebrews 12:14-15)
options: B. To follow peace with all men
C. To fast twice a week
questions.explanation: The text states 'One of the fundamental obligations of a Christian is to follow peace with all men (Hebrews 12:14-15).'

questions.question: Which verses are cited to show that as Christians, we should not only forgive but we should heal?
questions.explanation: The lesson links this to '(Genesis 45:1-15; 50:15-21)'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, forgiveness is merely a suggestion for Christians, not a command.
questions.explanation: The text says 'It is a command (Mark 11:25; Ephesians 4:32).'

questions.question: Why is it important to forgive based on Matthew 5:14 and 16, as explained in Outline I?
options: A. Because we are the light of the world and a living example that others follow
B. Because God will give us wealth
C. Because our enemies will fear us
D. Because we will avoid hell
questions.explanation: The text says 'We are the light of the world. We are a living example that others follow and watch consistently'.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that God is angry with us when we refuse to forgive?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly links 'God is angry with us when we refuse to forgive' to '(Matthew 18:32-35).'

questions.question: Lack of forgiveness hinders ________ (Mark 11:25).
questions.explanation: The lesson lists: 'iv. Lack of forgiveness hinders prayers (Mark 11:25).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline in the Week 7 lesson?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'ACHIEVING TRUE FORGIVENESS'.

questions.question: According to Outline II, forgiveness can help prevent which of the following?
options: B. Depression, anxiety, high blood pressure and hostility
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Forgiveness can help prevent depression, anxiety, high blood pressure and hostility (Romans 12:18).'

questions.question: Which scriptures are used to show that forgiveness boosts self-esteem and brings peace of mind?
questions.explanation: The text links this directly to '(Psalm 32:1-5; Hebrews 12:15b).'

questions.question: What is the acronym for the 5-step model adopted in the Week 7 lesson to achieve forgiveness?
questions.explanation: The lesson says 'we can adopt the programme known as the REACH model.'

questions.question: What does the 'R' in the REACH model stand for?
questions.explanation: The acronym REACH stands for Recall, Empathise, Altruistic Gift, Commit and Hold.

questions.question: In the REACH model, what does 'Recall' involve?
options: A. Being objective in understanding clearly the wrong done, without wallowing in self-pity
B. Remembering every bad thing the person ever did to you
C. Forgetting the wrong completely as if it never happened
D. Telling everyone what the person did
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Recall: To achieve forgiveness, be objective in understanding clearly the wrong done. It is not to wallow in self-pity or to hold a grudge...'

questions.question: In the REACH model, 'Empathise' means seeing from the offender's point of view and downplaying the wrong that was done.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly says 'without downplaying the wrong that was done'.

questions.question: What does the 'A' in the REACH model stand for?
questions.explanation: The 'A' stands for Altruistic gift.

questions.question: What happens during the 'Altruistic gift' stage of the REACH model?
options: A. The offended gives a physical gift to the offender
B. The offended remembers his shortcomings and realizes forgiveness is a gift we all need
D. The church offers a gift to reconcile the two parties
questions.explanation: The text says 'the offended can remember his shortcomings and how he was forgiven. He then realises that forgiveness is a gift we all need...'

questions.question: What does 'Commit' mean in the REACH model?
options: A. Committing the person into God's hands for judgement
B. Making public one's decision to forgive with an accountability partner
C. Committing to never speak to the person again
questions.explanation: The text says 'To commit is to make public (share) one’s decision to forgive with an accountability partner...'

questions.question: Which apostle wrote publicly about his commitment to have a clear conscience at all times (Acts 24:16), as cited in the 'Commit' stage?
questions.explanation: The text reads: 'Apostle Paul wrote publicly about his commitment... (Acts 24:16).'

questions.question: In the 'Hold' stage of the REACH model, when the feeling of hurt arises, you must remind yourself that you have forgiven and ultimately, you want the ________ of the offender.
questions.explanation: The text says 'remind yourself that you have forgiven and ultimately, you want the good of the offender'.

questions.question: The conclusion of the Week 7 lesson states that true forgiveness is cheap and easy.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states: 'True forgiveness may not come cheap, but its benefits outweigh the pain.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Then his brothers also went and fell down before his face, and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”'
questions.verse_text: Then his brothers also went and fell down before his face, and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”
questions.explanation: This is verse 18 from the main Bible Passage, Genesis 50:17-20.

questions.question: In the Bible Passage (Genesis 50:17-20), who was weeping when his brothers begged him for forgiveness?
questions.explanation: The text says 'And Joseph wept when they spoke to him.'

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Genesis 50:19), how did Joseph respond to his brothers when they fell down as his servants?
options: A. He threw them in prison
B. He said 'Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?'
C. He demanded they pay him back
D. He refused to speak to them
questions.explanation: Genesis 50:19 reads: 'Joseph said to them, “Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?'

questions.question: Joseph told his brothers: 'But as for you, you meant evil against me; but God meant it for ________, in order to bring it about as it is this day, to save many people alive.'
questions.explanation: Genesis 50:20 says 'but God meant it for good'.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that we all need forgiveness as an 'altruistic gift'?
questions.explanation: The 'Altruistic gift' section explicitly cites '(Colossians 3:13)'.

questions.question: According to the introduction, some believers say they will only be able to forgive when they get to heaven.
questions.explanation: The introduction states: 'Some believers even say it is when they get to heaven before they can forgive.'

questions.question: The 'Hold' stage of the REACH model acknowledges that memories of the painful event will never reoccur once you have truly forgiven.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'This is tough because memories of the event will often reoccur.'
